description: Near-miss probe (issue #183, docs/evaluation.md D1 near-miss set): counterparty inserts 'neither party is a Business Associate' language, which uses the words 'business associate' but never the literal trigger phrase 'business associate agreement' or the abbreviation 'BAA'. Must ACCEPT with zero fires on 'no-business-associate-agreement'.

must_not_flag.reason: The hunk uses 'Business Associate' as a defined-term reference, never the full phrase 'business associate agreement' or 'BAA'; it must not fire.

planted_variation.inserted_hunk: The parties acknowledge that neither party is a Business Associate of the other under HIPAA, and no business associate relationship is created by this Agreement.
planted_variation.notes: Near-miss: contains 'business associate' but never the two-word-boundary phrase 'business associate agreement', and never 'baa' as a standalone word.

detector_expectation.rationale: Hunk contains neither trigger_term 'business associate agreement' nor 'baa'.

authorship_note: AFK-drafted per issue #183 (owner-approved synthetic scaffold, 2026-07-10): fully invented, no real-world contract text, no de-identification required. Marked synthetic and pending GC sign-off; not production-qualified until Legal/GC reviews and approves.
